paper.title: Towards Autonomous Memory Agents
paper.abstract: Recent memory agents improve LLMs by extracting experiences and conversation history into an external storage. This enables low-overhead context assembly and online memory update without expensive LLM training. However, existing solutions remain passive and reactive; memory growth is bounded by information that happens to be available, while memory agents seldom seek external inputs in uncertainties. We propose autonomous memory agents that actively acquire, validate, and curate knowledge at a m

memory_insight.compression_quantization_methods: Not explicitly mentioned, but likely using techniques like knowledge graph compression or quantization
memory_insight.before_after_comparisons: U-Mem reduces memory usage by 14.6 points (HotpotQA) and 7.33 points (AIME25) compared to prior memory baselines

analysis.engineering_takeaway: Implementing a cost-aware knowledge-extraction cascade can significantly reduce memory usage and improve model performance
analysis.deployment_feasibility: Yes, but may require significant optimization for mobile devices due to limited RAM and processing power

review_questions: Is this genuinely a breakthrough? Or incremental improvement?
Is the DRAM impact claim supported by benchmarks?
